source.description: A reviewed six-panel pencil process contact sheet.
source.notes: One front-facing wooden easel with a small blank canvas on warm paper.

process_strategy: The sheet establishes the easel's triangular stance, then canvas, support shelf, crossbars, wood grain, color, and final cleanup without changing the structure.

final_elements.name: triangular easel frame
final_elements.description: A tall A-frame with two front legs and a center guide.

final_elements.name: small blank canvas
final_elements.description: A simple rectangular canvas centered in the frame.

final_elements.name: support shelf and back leg
final_elements.description: A shallow shelf under the canvas and a visible rear support leg.

final_elements.name: crossbars and wood grain
final_elements.description: A few structural crossbars and light grain marks.

final_elements.name: warm wood tint and floor shadow
final_elements.description: Pale brown pencil on the wooden parts and a soft base shadow.

frames.visible_job: Draws the tall triangular front frame and center guide.
Adds the blank rectangular canvas inside the established frame.
Adds the shelf and rear leg behind the existing canvas.
Adds crossbars and light grain marks to existing wooden members.
Adds pale wood tint and a soft floor shadow to established shapes.
introduces: warm wood tint and floor shadow

final_step.visible_job: Only reinforces existing legs, canvas edges, shelf, crossbars, grain, tint, and shadow.

rejection_checks: No leg, canvas, shelf, crossbar, grain, tint, or shadow may first appear in the final image.
The easel must retain one front-facing stance and canvas placement across all frames.
The blank canvas must remain unpainted, with no late illustration or extra studio prop added.
